purpose: Reference task graph for orchestrating the public healthcare Bronze, Silver, Gold staging, and AI Lakehouse load notebooks in Oracle AI Data Platform Workflow.
note: This is a workshop reference spec, not an OCI import artifact. Create the workflow in the AIDP UI using these task names, dependencies, task types, compute, and timeout values.

tasks.success_check: Bronze Delta outputs exist under /Volumes/e2eindustrydemos/default/e2eindustrydemovol/workshop_runs/<participant_id>/bronze for five CSV sources, one JSONL source, and one GeoJSON source.
Silver claims, facility, district, event, spatial, and population-health outputs are written under /Volumes/e2eindustrydemos/default/e2eindustrydemovol/workshop_runs/<participant_id>/silver.
Gold compatibility outputs are written under /Volumes/e2eindustrydemos/default/e2eindustrydemovol/workshop_runs/<participant_id>/gold_stage.
mpha_fact_claims_monthly and four Claims dimensions are current in the assigned AI Lakehouse target schema, for example goldailh.MPHA_P17.
All five Claims star schema tables have rows, orphan facts equal zero, and joined preview returns readable business rows.

incremental_talk_track: AIDP requires a timeout value on each task before the downstream chain is completed; use 60 minutes for this workshop.
Bronze appends or deduplicates new batches using batch_id, source file, ingestion timestamp, and natural keys.
Silver recomputes impacted records or partitions rather than rebuilding all conformed detail.
The AI Lakehouse load notebook reads existing target keys and inserts only new Claims dimension and monthly fact rows.
Validation gates decide whether OAC, ML, and agent workloads can consume the new Gold state.
